description: Cut-in pattern on a normal multilane road: ego travels in the center lane while a car from the right adjacent lane deterministically cuts into the ego lane on a collision course from the front-right. A follower is close behind the ego, making hard braking costly, and a pedestrian is present on the left escape side, making leftward avoidance contested rather than automatically preferred. The sweep varies actionable TTC, cut-in lateral speed, and left-side escape clearance to expose when braking, holding, or a constrained left evasive maneuver dominates.
hypothesis: For right-side cut-ins, left evasive/T-drift maneuvers dominate only when TTC and left escape clearance are sufficient; with short TTC, high cut-in lateral speed, or tight pedestrian-side clearance, braking/holding becomes preferable despite rear-end risk from the follower.
